# refactor(dataflow): Phase 5.10 — wire @classify redaction into Express read path

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `ba32ca525333` — refactor(dataflow): Phase 5.10 — wire @classify redaction into Express read path

**Body**:

Before this change, the ``@classify`` decorator (CARE-classified
fields, masking strategy, retention policy) attached metadata to model
classes but nothing in the Express or Workflow query path consulted
it. Every read returned the raw value regardless of the caller's
clearance — the redaction promise of ``MaskingStrategy.REDACT`` was a
silent lie.

This change wires the classification policy into the live read path
without breaking any of the 321 tests in the prior baseline.

**New surface in ``dataflow.classification.policy``**
- ``ClassificationPolicy.caller_can_access(field_level, clearance)`` —
  enforces the canonical clearance order PUBLIC < INTERNAL < SENSITIVE
  < PII < GDPR < HIGHLY_CONFIDENTIAL.
- ``ClassificationPolicy.apply_masking_strategy(value, strategy)`` —
  applies a single ``MaskingStrategy`` to a value: ``NONE`` /
  ``REDACT`` (``"[REDACTED]"``) / ``HASH`` (SHA-256 hex) /
  ``LAST_FOUR`` (mask all but last 4 chars) / ``ENCRYPT`` (sentinel
  ``"[ENCRYPTED]"``; true ciphertext is a storage-layer concern).
- ``ClassificationPolicy.apply_masking_to_record(model, record,
  clearance)`` and ``apply_masking_to_rows`` — mask classified fields
  on a single record or list of records based on caller clearance.
  Fields with classification at or below the caller's clearance pass
  through; fields above the clearance are replaced via the field's
  stored ``MaskingStrategy``. ``None`` clearance is treated as
  ``PUBLIC`` (most restrictive).

**New surface in ``dataflow.core.agent_context``**
- ``get_current_clearance`` / ``set_current_clearance`` and the
  scoped ``clearance_context`` / ``async_clearance_context`` context
  managers — mirror the agent context pattern from Phase 5.11.

**DataFlow wiring**
- ``DataFlow.__init__`` always instantiates a
  ``ClassificationPolicy``. The ``@db.model`` decorator now
  auto-registers any ``@classify`` metadata into the policy at model
  registration time. Models without ``@classify`` decorators are still
  registered (empty field set) and the read path short-circuits with
  zero overhead.
- ``DataFlow.classification_policy`` property exposes the policy for
  programmatic ``set_field`` calls when models cannot use the
  decorator (e.g., dynamically generated models).

**Express wiring**
- ``DataFlowExpress`` gains private
  ``_classify_enabled``/``_apply_classification_mask_record``/
  ``_apply_classification_mask_rows`` helpers that resolve the
  caller's clearance from ``clearance_context`` and apply masking
  via the policy.
- ``read``, ``list``, and ``find_one`` apply classification masking
  AFTER the Phase 5.11 trust PII filter and BEFORE caching/return,
  so cached entries already reflect the masking for the current
  clearance scope.

**Regression tests (19 new in test_phase_5_10_classification_redaction.py)**
- ``caller_can_access`` ordering across all six classification levels
- Every masking strategy: NONE, REDACT, ENCRYPT, HASH (SHA-256
  byte-equality), LAST_FOUR (long and short input), None value
  pass-through
- ``apply_masking_to_record`` masks fields above caller clearance
- None clearance is treated as PUBLIC
- ``apply_masking_to_record`` is a no-op on unclassified models
- ``apply_masking_to_rows`` masks every row independently
- DataFlow has a ``classification_policy`` property by default
- ``@db.model`` auto-registers ``@classify`` metadata
- Express ``read`` masks classified fields for PUBLIC caller and
  reveals them under ``async_clearance_context(PII)``
- Express ``list`` masks each row
- Express ``find_one`` masks classified fields and unmasks under
  the right clearance
- Models without ``@classify`` are unchanged

**Test results**
- 321 prior tests: still pass
- 19 new Phase 5.10 regression tests: pass
- Total: 340 passed, zero regressions

**Unblocks**
- Downstream workspaces can mark sensitive columns with ``@classify``
  and trust that low-clearance callers see masked values without any
  application-level redaction code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
